prestazioni: Engine swap to M110.986 (3.5L inline-6 from the W116 350SE) with forged Mahle pistons (8.8:1 compression), Schrick 284° camshafts, and a custom 3.7L stroker kit using a billet crankshaft from JP Performance, paired with a Kugelfischer mechanical fuel injection system recalibrated by K-Jetronic specialist Pierburg for 220+ hp at 6,000 RPM

Full drivetrain reinforcement with a Getrag 280/5 5-speed manual transmission (from the W123 280CE) adapted via a custom bellhousing by Gbox, limited-slip differential from a W126 500SEL with 3.69:1 gearing, and a Sachs Performance twin-disc clutch with cerametallic friction plates

Suspension overhaul using Bilstein B8 Sport dampers with custom valving for the W116’s recirculating-ball steering geometry, Eibach Pro-Kit springs (20mm drop), polyurethane bushings from Meyle HD for the front A-arms and rear subframe, and a custom sway bar setup from H&R (24mm front, 18mm rear) to reduce body roll in high-speed cornering
estetica: Full OEM+ restoration with period-correct Euro-spec bumpers (chrome with integrated fog lights from the W116 450SEL 6.9), Hella 170mm headlights with Cibié H4 bulbs, and a custom front splitter from a W126 560SEL lip adapted by Kamei for the W116’s grille

Interior retrim in Nappa leather from Bridge of Weir (original MB-Tex pattern) with contrasting black stitching, Recaro SRD front seats (from the W123 280CE) modified with custom mounting brackets, and a MOMO Prototipo steering wheel (380mm) with a quick-release hub from NRG Innovations

Exterior accents including a custom rear ducktail spoiler from Zender (originally designed for the W123 but adapted for the W116’s trunk lid), BBS RS 15x8 wheels with 205/60R15 Michelin XWX tires, and a full respray in Mercedes-Benz Classic Signal Red (DB 568) with a single-stage lacquer finish by Glasurit
